title: "These Children Can Read Your Mind!” (ft. Ky Dickens | Telepathy Tapes Documentary)
summary: This interview features Ky Dickens, documentary filmmaker behind 'The Telepathy Tapes,' discussing her investigation into non-verbal autistic children who allegedly demonstrate telepathic abilities. She describes controlled experiments with children like Mia and Akil who accurately read their parents' thoughts while blindfolded, and explores the broader implications for consciousness, communication rights, and the afterlife.

acronyms.definition: A technique for teaching non-verbal autistic individuals to communicate by pointing to letters
The board that gives accreditation to speech language pathologists; has controversially labeled spelling-based communication methods as pseudoscience
Perception occurring independently of known sensory processes
The cinematographer responsible for camera and lighting in film production

events.description: Psychical studies began with the Society for Psychical Research
Dr. Diane Powell met Oliver Sacks and learned about autism and savant abilities

key_claims.claim: Non-verbal autistic children demonstrated 100% accuracy in telepathy tests involving random numbers and images while blindfolded
Every non-speaker has telepathic gifts according to the non-verbal individuals tested
Non-verbal autistic individuals communicate telepathically through a 'hill' - a telepathic chat room where they can tune in and communicate with each other
Sign language took approximately 150 years to be accepted as a valid form of communication
Non-speaking autism is a motor control issue, not an intelligence issue - the individuals are intellectually capable but cannot control their bodies
Oliver Sacks documented autistic twins who could recite consecutive six-digit prime numbers back and forth with no known algorithm
Josiah, a young non-verbal autistic boy, knew private details about author Max Davis including activities in his home and writing process without any prior communication
Akil can perform telepathy with his mother across the room without physical contact
